title: Oyu'baat
content:
{{Top|leg|italics=1}}
{{Structure
|image=
|name=''Oyu'baat''
|constructed=Prior to the [[Mandalorian Wars]]<ref name="ImpCom501st">''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]''</ref>
|destroyed=
|type=
|location=[[Keldabe/Legends|Keldabe]], [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]]<ref name="O66">''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]''</ref>
|interest=
|rebuilt=
|builder=
|height=Three stories<ref name="TLJ">[[The Last Jedi (novel)|''The Last Jedi'']]</ref>
|width=
|affiliation=[[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]]<ref name="Sacrifice">''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]''</ref>
}}
{{Quote|Everyone knows the ''Oyu'baat''. It's been here since Canderous Ordo was a glint in his mama's eye, and it never closes, ever.|[[Parja Bralor]]|<ref name="O66" />}}
The '''''Oyu'baat''''' was an ancient [[Hotel/Legends|hotel]] and [[Tapcafe/Legends|tapcaf]], located on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]]. Situated in Mandalore's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]], [[Keldabe/Legends|Keldabe]], the ''Oyu'baat'' was the oldest [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] on the planet, open for several thousand [[Standard year/Legends|years]] by the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|start]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Named for the word ''oyu'baat'', meaning "[[Universe/Legends|universe]]" in the [[Mando'a/Legends|''Mando'a'']] [[Language/Legends|language]], the [[Time/Legends|time]]-worn tapcaf served as an unofficial center for the loose government formed by the [[Chief/Legends|chieftains]] of the various [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Soldier/Legends|warrior]] clans, who were without formal palaces or designated government buildings. It was not uncommon to find clan leaders among the ''Oyu'baat''{{'s}} other customers, meeting and brokering deals around the cantina's [[Fire/Legends|fire]], or over [[Alcohol/Legends|drinks]]. The ''Oyu'baat'' brewed [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Black ale/Legends|''ne'tra gal'']] [[Ale/Legends|ale]] on site, and served a variety of [[Foodstuff/Legends|foods]] and other drinks. As entertainment, the tapcaf showed [[Limmie/Legends|bolo-ball]] matches on a large [[Holovid/Legends|holovid]] [[Viewscreen/Legends|screen]], and offered table games of ''[[cu'bikad]]'' for patrons.

In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], Mandalorian soldier [[Fenn Shysa]] met with the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|clone deserter]] [[Fi Skirata]] at the ''Oyu'baat'', seeking to convince the wounded [[Clone commando/Legends|commando]] to act as [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']] until one could legitimately be appointed. Skirata was unreceptive to the idea, and Shysa eventually accepted the position of ''Mand'alor'', himself; celebrations were held at the ''Oyu'baat'' when word of the news spread. When the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] was first establishing a [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] on land leased to them outside of Keldabe, Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] were banned from coming into the ''Oyu'baat'', due to the threat to commerce posed by Mandalorians unwilling to remove their concealing [[Armor/Legends|helmets]] in the presence of the [[Troop/Legends|troopers]]. Around [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]], the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]-in-hiding [[Jax Pavan]] came to the ''Oyu'baat'' seeking information from a [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] by the name of [[Tyno Fabris]], who was living in Keldabe at the time. Pavan made several visits to the Mandalorian establishment during the course of his stay on Mandalore as he attempted to make use of the gangster's resources in locating a [[Thi Xon Yimmon|friend]] who'd been captured by the Empire. During the last of these visits, the ''Oyu'baat'' suffered minor damage when a [[Skirmish in the Oyu'baat|firefight]] broke out between Pavan and [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]] of the recently arrived Black Sun [[Vigo/Legends|Vigo]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]], who had gone back on a deal that had been struck between Black Sun and the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. Pavan was forced to flee the cantina through a [[Window/Legends|window]], [[Telekinesis/Legends|blasting]] it open with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and knocking free a section of the surrounding wall.

The damage to the ''Oyu'baat''{{'s}} structure was subsequently repaired, and the ancient tapcaf outlasted the Empire's [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]]-long occupation and subjugation of Mandalore, which finally came to an end when Fenn Shysa and his [[Army/Legends|army]] of [[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]] drove the Imperials from the [[Mandalore sector/Legends|Mandalore sector]]. The ''Oyu'baat'' also survived the [[Battle of Mandalore (Yuuzhan Vong War)|damage]] incurred to Mandalore by the [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] when they struck at the Mandalorian [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. In [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], the cantina came to share its role as a favored meeting place of the clans with [[MandalMotors Hall]], a newly constructed communal building donated for public use by the [[MandalMotors/Legends|MandalMotors]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] company. During this time, the ''Oyu'baat'' saw frequent visits from reigning ''Mand'alor'' [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] and his granddaughter, [[Mirta Gev]], and was the site of much celebration when a new lode of [[Beskar/Legends|''beskar'']] [[Iron/Legends|iron]] [[Ore/Legends|ore]] was discovered near the town of [[Enceri]], promising to bring about a new era of economic prosperity for the warrior clans. During the [[Galactic Cup of Limmie|galactic bolo-ball tournament]] of [[41 ABY]], numerous Mandalorians flocked to the ''Oyu'baat'' to watch the games on the bar's viewscreen, though one viewing session was interrupted by the arrival of [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] and the revelation that she was a Jedi Knight.

==Description==
{{Quote|Someone better tell him the ''Oyu'baat'' is as five-star as we get in Keldabe.|[[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], referring to the [[Coruscanti/Legends|Coruscanti]] [[Doctor/Legends|doctor]], [[Beluine]]|<ref name="Revelation">''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]''</ref>}}
The ''Oyu'baat'' was located on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]], within the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Keldabe/Legends|Keldabe]]. Situated at one end of an ancient paved square, across from [[Chortav Meshurkaane]],<ref name="ImpCom501st" /> the ''Oyu'baat'' was a [[Hotel/Legends|hotel]] and [[Tapcafe/Legends|tapcaf]] housed within a large,<ref name="Revelation" /> three-story structure<ref name="TLJ" /> that appeared to some as a gathering of smaller buildings that had merged over [[Time/Legends|time]].<ref name="O66" /> The ''Oyu'baat'' was constructed almost entirely of [[Tree/Legends|wood]] and stone, with a sloped, tiled roof, beneath which a massive wooden ridgepole—as wide in diameter as three average [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|men]]—could be seen jutting out on either side from below the building's eaves.<ref name="TLJ" /> A pair of doors stood at the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina's]] entrance<ref name="O66" /> beneath a large portico. The ''Oyu'baat''{{'s}} exterior facade was coated with painted [[plaster]] that was known to chip and flake with age,<ref name="TLJ" /> and possessed an eclectic assortment of [[Window/Legends|windows]] with non-perpendicular angles and a general disregard for being level.<ref name="O66" /> The ''Oyu'baat'' took its name from the [[Mando'a/Legends|''Mando'a'']] word ''oyu'baat'', a term which translated to "[[Universe/Legends|universe]]" in [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]].<ref name="Mando'a">{{WebCite|url=http://www.karentraviss.com/page20/page26/index.html|text=Mando'a|author=Karen Traviss|work=[http://www.karentraviss.com Karen Traviss]|archivedate=20110925083955}}</ref> The name in both [[Language/Legends|languages]] was written on a sign outside of the tapcaf's entrance. The dual-language sign also warned that [[strill]]s,<ref name="Revelation" /> six-[[Limb/Legends|legged]] [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] animals [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to Mandalore,<ref name="ImpCom501st" /> were not allowed inside, and notified potential customers that the cantina accepted barter as means of [[Currency/Legends|payment]],<ref name="Revelation" /> in addition to standard [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].<ref name="TLJ" />

Inside the doors of the tapcaf, a broad but shallow staircase led into the ''Oyu'baat''{{'s}} main hall, an immense room built primarily from dark-colored wood. Tables for customers to sit at were spread about the hall at intervals, while booths lined the outer walls, each with a sliding wooden screen that could be drawn across the booth's opening for optional added privacy. Two curved bars<ref name="TLJ" /> with long counters and a number of available seats<ref name="ImpCom501st" /> were the centerpiece of the ''Oyu'baat''{{'s}} main chamber: one was allocated to serving [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]], while the other offered a variety of beverages.<ref name="TLJ" /> The ''Oyu'baat'' kept a large stock of [[Mandallian Narcolethe/Legends|Mandallian Narcolethe]], and brewed its own [[Black ale/Legends|''ne'tra gal'']], a sweet black Mandalorian [[Ale/Legends|ale]]<ref name="Revelation" /> they served to customers in [[Glass/Legends|glass]] mugs.<ref name="ImpCom501st" /> Among its non-[[Alcohol/Legends|alcoholic]] beverage selection, the ''Oyu'baat'' sold a blend of [[spiced caf]] for which the cantina was well known even among non-Mandalorians.<ref name="TLJ" /> The tapcaf prepared soups and a number of other food dishes, including a meat and [[Vegetable/Legends|vegetable]] [[Stew/Legends|stew]]. [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] folk humor insisted that the same stew had been left to simmer for [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]], only with fresh meat and vegetables thrown in daily,<ref name="O66" /> and it was said that the ''Oyu'baat''{{'s}} menu had remained the same since the time of the [[Mandalorian Wars]].<ref name="ImpCom501st" /> During the [[Standard day/Legends|day]], the bar area was bathed in [[Star/Legends|sunlight]] from the skylights housed in the roof,<ref name="TLJ" /> and behind the bar, the current [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]]-hunting list was displayed for Mandalorian patrons who pursued [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] work, as both a [[Holodisplay/Legends|holodisplay]]<ref name="O66" /> and with [[Flimsiplast/Legends|flimsi]] posters.<ref name="ImpCom501st" />

At the far end of the main room, opposite to the ''Oyu'baat''{{'s}} entrance,<ref name="TLJ" /> was a large, open log [[Fire/Legends|fire]].<ref name="Sacrifice" /> The fire was surrounded by a wide alcove that could host more than a dozen individuals,<ref name="TLJ" /> and was a particularly popular choice for patrons to gather around.<ref name="Sacrifice" /><ref name="O66" /> A noisy, automated hot-air unit provided additional heat to rest of the ''Oyu'baat''.<ref name="ImpCom501st" /> The ''Oyu'baat''{{'s}} interior was clean, yet rustic,<ref name="Revelation" /> decorated with vivid tapestries depicting various events and figures from the Mandalorian culture's long history; many favored shades of deep red in their composition.<ref name="TLJ" /> The ancient cantina's main hall was often noisy,<ref name="TLJ" /> and featured a large [[Holovid/Legends|holovid]] [[Viewscreen/Legends|viewscreen]] commonly used to watch sporting events, in addition to boards for playing ''[[cu'bikad]]'',<ref name="Revelation" /> a rough Mandalorian table game.<ref name="O66" /> Broad galleries overlooked the large main chamber from the ''Oyu'baat''{{'s}} second and third floors.<ref name="TLJ" /> Part hotel, the ''Oyu'baat''{{'s}} upper levels had a number of rooms interested parties could rent, for overnight periods or longer,<ref name="Revelation" /> and were reached by a staircase at the rear of the main hall. The stairway also concealed a hidden panel which led to a secret, office-sized space within the ''Oyu'baat'' that few knew about.<ref name="TLJ" /> The cantina smelled of the wood fire, yeast from the brewing ale, and the scents of various cooking foods.<ref name="O66" /> With the exception of rare special occasions,<ref name="TLJ" /><ref name="Revelation" /> the ''Oyu'baat'' was always open for business.<ref name="O66" />

==History==
===Early history===
{{Quote|Fenn Shysa's just accepted the ''kyr'bes''. He's our new ''Mand'alor''. The ale's flowing in the ''Oyu'baat''.|A Mandalorian man speaking to [[Kal Skirata]]|<ref name="O66" />}}
Constructed in Mandalore's capital city of Keldabe prior to the start of the Mandalorian Wars,<ref name="ImpCom501st" /> a [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]-spanning war of conquest between the Mandalorian warrior clans under [[Mandalore the Ultimate]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]],<ref name="TEGW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> the ''Oyu'baat'' was the oldest cantina on the planet Mandalore.<ref name="ImpCom501st" /> The ''Oyu'baat''{{'s}} prominence made it a convenient meeting place for Mandalorians in the Keldabe area, and the tapcaf became an unofficial center for the loose government formed by the Mandalorian warrior clans.<ref name="O66" /> Due to Mandalore's lack of formal government buildings, clan [[Chief/Legends|chieftains]] often met at the ''Oyu'baat'' to discuss matters and broker deals, usually over [[Alcohol/Legends|drinks]].<ref name="Sacrifice" /><ref name="ImpCom501st" /> Over the ages, the ''Oyu'baat'' also developed a degree of notability among non-Mandalorians, along with the blend of [[Spice/Legends|spiced]] [[Caf/Legends|caf]] the cantina served. Throughout its history, the ''Oyu'baat''{{'s}} outer plaster facade had been painted in a variety of colors, including brown, pale grey, and a vivid orange<ref name="TLJ" />—a color traditionally associated with the concept of ''shereshoy'', or a lust for life, in the Mandalorian culture.<ref name="ImpCom501st" /> By the time of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], a conflict between the Galactic Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]],<ref name="TEGW" /> the cantina's plastered exterior had begun to succumb to excessive wear, with chunks falling from the walls and revealing the ''Oyu'baat''{{'s}} multi-layered history.<ref name="TLJ" />

In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the ''Oyu'baat'' was staffed by a [[Unidentified Oyu'baat bartender|male bartender]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], the cantina was selected as a meeting place between chieftain [[Fenn Shysa]] and the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] deserter, [[Fi Skirata]]. There, Shysa attempted to persuade the [[Clone commando/Legends|clone soldier]] to act as former [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett's]] heir until a new ''Mand'alor'' could be selected. However, Skirata was still recovering from [[Brain/Legends|brain]] trauma suffered during a [[Battle of Gaftikar|mission]] he had been apart of while still in service to the Republic, and with the support of girlfriend [[Parja Bralor]], declined Shysa's request. Though Shysa was sympathetic to Skirata's plight, violence broke out when the former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]-turned-Mandalorian [[Bardan Jusik]], arrived at the ''Oyu'baat''. There to meet with Skirata and Bralor, Jusik was accosted by the former [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|ARC trooper]] deserter, [[Sull]], who recognized Jusik as his former [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] and confronted him with accusations. When it seemed as if Sull might have drawn his [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster]] on Jusik, Skirata [[Unarmed combat/Legends|punched]] the [[Alpha-class Advanced Recon Commando|''Alpha'' ARC]] reflexively, and Shysa was forced to break up the scuffle. Later that year, Fi Skirata's [[Gai bal manda|adopted]] [[Parent/Legends|father]], [[Kal Skirata]], met with Shysa at the ''Oyu'baat'' to discuss the future of Mandalore in the wake of the [[Declaration of a New Order|transformation]] of the Republic into the new [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and their leasing of land on Mandalore for a base. At this time, Skirata's was one of many names on the bounty list projected behind the ''Oyu'baat''{{'s}} bar. During the meeting, Skirata suggested Shysa take up the role of ''Mand'alor'', which Shysa accepted a short time after, in the face of the arrival of the Imperial [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]]; celebrations were held at the ''Oyu'baat'' once word of Shysa's ascension spread.<ref name="O66" />

===Imperial occupation===
{{Quote|Don't worry. Imperials don't come in here. Made sure of it.|The [[Unidentified Oyu'baat bartender|''Oyu'baat''{{'s}} bartender]]|<ref name="ImpCom501st" />}}
As the Empire arrived on Mandalore, the ''Oyu'baat''{{'s}} [[Bartender/Legends|bartender]] secured a deal with the Imperials to keep them from entering the cantina, fearful of the business he stood to loose if prospective patrons were unwilling to remove their [[Armor/Legends|helmets]] in the presence of the Empire's [[Troop/Legends|troops]]. Three weeks after the rise of the Galactic Empire, the Mandalorian friends [[Jarkyc]] and [[Hayar]] came to the ''Oyu'baat'', celebrating a con they had pulled on the [[Unidentified Imperial commander (Mandalore)|commander]] of the Imperial garrison on Mandalore. The pair's loud laughter drew the attention of fellow patrons, including Bardan Jusik and [[Ordo Skirata]], who had stopped into the ''Oyu'baat'' for a drink. When Jusik inquired as to what had them laughing so hard, Jarkyc explained that [[Hayar's brother]] had built an [[City of Bone|adventure theme park]] to attract offworld tourists, though it had failed and never opened. When the Imperial [[Commander/Legends|commander]] took an interest in the park, shaped like an enormous [[Mythosaur/Legends|mythosaur]] [[Skeleton/Legends|skeleton]], Jarkyc and his comrades told the Imperials that the park was actually a Mandalorian temple with great cultural significance; wanting to make a statement to Mandalore's inhabitants, the commander bought the failed park from them and installed the garrison and base on what he falsely believed to be sacred ground. By large, the ''Oyu'baat''{{'s}} many patrons joined Jarkyc and Hayar in laughter at the story. Several weeks later, Kal Skirata and [[Walon Vau]], stopped into the ''Oyu'baat'' for drinks while discussing the reappearance of their old ''[[Cuy'val Dar]]'' associates [[Dred Priest]] and [[Isabet Reau]] on Mandalore with ties to the renegade [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]] faction.<ref name="ImpCom501st" />

In the aftermath of the [[New Mandalorians/Legends|New Mandalorians]]' [[Takeover of Mandalore/Legends|fall]] and the dissolution of the criminal [[Shadow Collective/Legends|Shadow Collective]], elements of the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] syndicate had moved to fill the void the fleeting criminal contingent had left. By [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]], the syndicate established a known presence on Mandalore's moon, [[Concordia/Legends|Concordia]], and [[Tyno Fabris]]—[[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] to the Black Sun [[Vigo/Legends|Vigo]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]]—had taken up residence in Keldabe, where the [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanian]] gangster worked out of an office in the ''Oyu'baat'' cantina. Around 18 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]], the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]-in-hiding [[Jax Pavan]] journeyed to Mandalore in search of information on the missing leader of [[Whiplash]], [[Thi Xon Yimmon]], who had been captured by the Empire. During his time on the Mandalorian homeworld, he met with Tyno Fabris at the ''Oyu'baat'' several times while attempting to enlist Black Sun's aid in his mission, occasionally stopping for a drink at the bar and engaging the ''Oyu'baat''{{'s}} [[Balosar (species)/Legends|Balosar]] [[Waiter/Legends|waitress]] [[Tlinetha]] in conversation. Though he initially approached Fabris under an assumed name, Pavan's true identity came to light when Fabris contacted Vigo Xizor about the deal being brokered; Xizor, who had previous dealings with Pavan, journeyed to Mandalore to meet with the fugitive Jedi at the ''Oyu'baat'' in person. The two initially came to an arrangement, but Xizor later went back upon his word after reassessing the risk of Imperial reprisal Black Sun would be taking by aiding the Jedi recover the leader of Whiplash. [[Skirmish in the Oyu'baat|Fighting]] broke out between Pavan and Xixor's Mandalorian [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]] when the Vigo has his [[Brank|men]] turn on the Jedi, forcing Pavan to flee the cantina by way of a window he [[Telekinesis/Legends|broke out]] using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], inadvertently knocking a section of the surrounding wall free as he made his escape.<ref name="TLJ" /> The damage to the ''Oyu'baat'' was later repaired,<ref name="Sacrifice" /> and Pavan was able to depart Mandalore unharmed.<ref name="TLJ" />

===Mandalore's resurgence===
{{Quote|Goran just commed to say he's down at the ''Oyu'baat'' and you're not going to believe what showed up in an X-wing.''"<br />"''What?''"<br />"''A Jedi. He thinks its Jaina Solo.|[[Medrit Vasur]] to Boba Fett|<ref name="Revelation" />}}
The ''Oyu'baat'' outlasted<ref name="Sacrifice" /> the [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]]-long occupation of Mandalore by the Empire's forces, who were eventually [[Battle of the City of Bone|overcome]] and driven from [[Mandalore sector/Legends|Mandalorian space]] by Fenn Shysa and his [[Army/Legends|army]] of [[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]].<ref name="GaW">''[[Galaxy at War]]''</ref> The ages-old cantina also survived the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], weathering the [[Battle of Mandalore (Yuuzhan Vong War)|devastation]] Mandalore incurred at the [[Limb/Legends|hands]] of the [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Species/Legends|race]] in an attempt to destroy the several thousand-year-old warrior culture. However, the ''Oyu'baat''{{'s}} longstanding role as meeting place for the clans' leaders was encroached upon in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]] by the construction of [[MandalMotors Hall]], a [[Granite/Legends|granite]] communal building donated to the community by the [[MandalMotors/Legends|MandalMotors]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] company.<ref name="Sacrifice" />

Following the discovery of a previously untapped lode of [[Beskar/Legends|''beskar'']] [[Iron/Legends|iron]] [[Ore/Legends|ore]] north of the small Mandalore town of [[Enceri]], numerous Mandalorians flocked to the ''Oyu'baat'' to celebrate the economic boost the iron find would provide Mandalore. Reigning ''Mand'alor'' [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] attended the gathering, as it had become a habit to visit the ancient cantina at least once a weak, as a means of keeping spirits high among the Mandalorian patrons. That night, Fett's friend [[Goran Beviin]] discussed news from the wider galaxy with a retinue of associates, while several ''Oyu'baat'' customers—including [[Baltan Carid]], Beviin's [[Marriage/Legends|husband]] and daughter, [[Medrit Vasur]] and [[Dinua Jeban]], and Fett's granddaughter [[Mirta Gev]]—played ''cu'bikad'' at one of the bar's tables. Later, Fett and his granddaughter Gev met with [[Venku Skirata]] and an elderly Bardan Jusik, now going by the name "Gotab," at the ''Oyu'baat''. Using his [[Light side of the Force/Legends|Force]] [[Psychometry/Legends|abilities]], Gotab read a broken [[Heart of Fire|heart-of-fire]] pendant for Fett that used to belong to his ex-wife, [[Sintas Vel]]. Due to the painfully private nature of the reading, Fett used his authority as ''Mand'alor'' to order the bar evacuated of other patrons until their business was concluded.<ref name="Sacrifice" />

By [[41 ABY]], [[Cham Detta]] had become the bartender of the ''Oyu'baat''. Frequent customer Baltan Carid joked during this time that, despite the ''Oyu'baat''{{'s}} policy forbidding strills from entering the premises, the hotel and tapcaf's executive suite was occupied by a strill. Boba Fett's personal [[Doctor/Legends|doctor]], [[Beluine]], was arranged to stay in a room at the ''Oyu'baat'' during his time on Mandalore. When Jedi Knight [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] traveled to Mandalore that year, seeking the aid of Fett in stopping her [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] brother, [[Darth Caedus]], she landed at the Keldabe [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] and was promptly taken to the ''Oyu'baat'' to meet with Fett. Solo's arrival at the cantina came in the midst of a [[Limmie/Legends|bolo-ball]] [[Galactic Cup of Limmie|tournament]] that a number of Mandalorians had come to the ''Oyu'baat'' to watch on the bar's large viewscreen; when she was outed as a Jedi by passerby Gotab, the tapcaf's patrons drew their [[Blaster/Legends|weapons]] on Solo, until she offered an explanation for her presence. Upon meeting with Solo at the ''Oyu'baat'' several minutes later, Fett agreed to teach her and instructed Solo to take a room at the ''Oyu'baat'' for the duration of her stay, though Goran Beviin instead offered her the hospitality of his [[Clan Beviin|family's]] [[Beviin-Vasur farm|farm]]. On the day of his granddaughter's [[Wedding of Ghes Orade and Mirta Gev|marriage]] to Mandalorian soldier [[Ghes Orade]], Fett met with Imperial [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Natasi Daala]] at the ''Oyu'baat'': the two were alone, as most of the Mandalorians who would have otherwise been present were instead celebrating the nuptials at [[Novoc Vevut's home|the home]] of Ghes Orade's [[Novoc Vevut|father]], including the bartender. Fett and Daala talked of their pasts, and discussed the future, before departing to attend the marital celebration of Fett's granddaughter.<ref name="Revelation" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Oyu'baat'' first appeared in ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]] in the [[2007]] ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]'' novel, ''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]'' by [[Author/Legends|author]] [[Karen Traviss]].<ref name="Sacrifice" /> Traviss again made use of the ''Oyu'baat'' as a locale in her fourth and fifth entries to the [[Republic Commando|''Republic'' and ''Imperial Commando'' series]], entitled [[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel|''Order 66'']]<ref name="O66" /> and ''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]'',<ref name="ImpCom501st" /> in addition to her third ''Legacy of the Force'' novel, ''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]''.<ref name="Revelation" /> The [[2013]] novel [[The Last Jedi (novel)|''The Last Jedi'']], co-written by [[Michael Reaves]] and [[Maya Kaathryn Bohnhoff]], again featured the ''Oyu'baat''.<ref name="TLJ" />

==Appearances==
*''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]''
*''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]''
*[[The Last Jedi (novel)|''The Last Jedi'']]
*''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]'' {{1st}}
*''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]''

==Notes and references==
{{Reflist}}
